title: Successful Treatment of Charcot-Marie-Tooth Chronic Pain with Spinal Cord Stimulation: A Case Study
journal_name: Neuromodulation: Technology at the Neural Interface
study_question: Can spinal cord stimulation relieve chronic pain from Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease?
population_assessed: 37-year-old man with Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease and chronic lower extremity pain over 20 years.
outcome_measures: VAS, SF-McGill Pain Questionnaire, Pain Disability Index, Oswestry Disability Questionnaire, SF-36, medication use
inclusion_criteria: CMT diagnosis, chronic intractable lower extremity pain, failed conservative therapy, passed psychological assessment.
answer_to_study_question: Spinal cord stimulation reduced pain, disability, medication use, and improved quality of life at six months.
reasoning: Most fields showed strong consensus across all models. Chose Anthropic's volume/issue over conflicting lower-plausibility entries from others. Merged outcome and inclusion details from consistent high-confidence extractions.
